description.task: Compute the minimum number of single-character edits (insertions, deletions, substitutions) required to transform one string into another. Use bottom-up DP where dp[i][j] = edit distance between a[0..i-1] and b[0..j-1].
instructions: horse→ros: delete h, delete r (reorder), delete e → 3 edits
Edit distance is used in spell checkers, DNA sequence comparison, diff tools
description.expectedOutput:
horse -> ros:         3
intention -> execution: 5
abc -> abc:            0
abc -> :               3

description.task: Find the contiguous subarray (containing at least one number) with the maximum sum. Use Kadane's algorithm (a DP variant): track the maximum sum ending at each position. Also return the start and end indices of the max subarray.
instructions: arr={-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4}: max subarray is [4,-1,2,1] with sum=6, indices 3-6
All-negative array: max subarray is the single least-negative element [-1]=−1
Kadane's is O(n) time with O(1) space
description.expectedOutput:
Max sum [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4]: 6
Max sum=6, start=3, end=6
All negatives [-3,-1,-2]: -1

description.task: Given a string, find the minimum number of cuts needed to partition it so that every substring is a palindrome. Use DP: first precompute a 2D palindrome table, then compute the minimum cuts.
instructions: The palindrome precomputation and base cases are already provided
aab: cut after aa → aa|b = 1 cut; abacaba is a palindrome itself → 0 cuts
abcde: all single chars → 4 cuts needed
